MEASURED: 36,844 lines of Lean vs ~4,200 lines of prose ABOUT the project. 9,216 comment lines = 29.4% of non-blank Lean.

THE FOUR DOCS THAT WERE ACTIVELY WRONG (not merely stale):
1. README.md advertised a REFUTED theorem — 'So Ch(K) and V are homotopy equivalent!' — while LabelSpace.lean:237 says that step 'fails for branching K' and Testing/TwoSquares.lean is the machine-checked counterexample. (User has since rewritten the README themselves.)
2. CLAUDE.md contradicted itself 30 lines apart: :14 'MEMORY.md is the live status board, keep it updated' vs :44 'do NOT use MEMORY.md files'.
3. Schedule/DESIGN.md asserted lemma L7 at :289 having REFUTED it at :262.
4. Salvetti/BRAID.md's headline object G K := ⨆ ConcGrpd(K^⊗n) ⋊ Sₙ does not exist in the code (no ⋊, no K^⊗n, no BrMonCat anywhere). Superseded by BraidFunctor.lean's ConcCatN, which grades by event count in ONE K. SUPERSEDED banner added; the rebase-or-retire call is Cubical-w7x, left open because it is a MATH decision tied to the paper.
Plus: a memory auto-loaded every session claimed the repo was sorry-free 'except Research/Conjectures.lean' — that folder does not exist and there are zero sorries.

STRUCTURAL FIX: tracked files pointed into ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/, a machine-local dir that is NOT in git — including a LEAN SOURCE FILE (Chains/Segal.lean:817, 'See [[cubechains-segal]]'). Anyone cloning the repo got dangling pointers into one laptop. All removed.

DIAGNOSIS of why this happened (the real deliverable): the junk prose is DEFENSIVE, not explanatory — 'sorry-free' x10, 'unconditional' x7 in one file, 'assumption-free' x7 in another. All true, all worthless to a reader, all addressed to the requester. It is a status report to a grader, misfiled into a source file. Prior instruction attempts failed because they were PROHIBITIONS WITHOUT DESTINATIONS: 'don't write process prose' lost to 'leave the next agent enough context', which had a concrete affordance (a markdown file) while the prohibition had none. Beads supplies the missing destination.

THE RULE, now in CLAUDE.md: if a fact would become false when a bead closes, it belongs in the bead, not in a file. Plus three MECHANICAL tests (would it become false on close? does it have a tense? does it read as an argument that your work is good?), budgets not adjectives (<=1 line of intent per declaration, <=10 per module docstring), and a destinations table. Key correction discovered mid-work: bd remember is NOT git-portable (issues.jsonl exports only _type:issue; the Dolt DB is gitignored; a clone fetches refs/heads/* only) — so durable knowledge that must survive a clone goes in a PINNED BEAD or in git, and CLAUDE.md stays the constitution because it is the only thing that loads when bd is absent.

Remaining work is tracked under Cubical-ndf. Open: w7x (BRAID.md, needs the user's math call).

title: Audit the true import cone of the braid result
description:
Timeboxed. Compute what hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure (Salvetti/PurityHDA.lean) actually transitively imports. The §8.1 claim (Foundations + Chains + Arrangements + Salvetti + 3 Schedule files) is a BELIEF, not a measurement. Verify it before moving any file.

Watch for landmine #1: an import of equivWedgeCat/refineToWedge anywhere in the cone means a side condition (NonSelfLinked / AdmitsAltitude) is silently present and the 'unconditional' claim is at risk. Check with #print axioms on the headline theorem.

DONE WHEN: a written file list (the cone), plus a diff of it against the §8.1 claim, plus #print axioms output for hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure.
close_reason:
AUDIT DONE. Two headline findings.

1. UNCONDITIONALITY: CONFIRMED, by Lean rather than by reading imports.
   hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure : ∀ (K : BPSet), HasGlobalEventNaming K ↔ BraidPure K
   axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. Same for braidFunctor.
   Landmine #1 (equivWedgeCat silently carrying NSL/AdmitsAltitude) CANNOT be present: a
   hypothesis-free statement that compiles sorry-free with no extra axioms has nowhere to hide a
   side condition. Importing a file that merely CONTAINS conditional theorems is harmless. The
   import cone is therefore irrelevant to unconditionality — it only matters for the file surgery.

2. THE §8.1 CLAIM IS WRONG. Cone = 58 of 124 files: Salvetti 18, Chains 12, SCHEDULE 11,
   Arrangements 10, Foundations 7. §8.1 says the braid result needs THREE Schedule files
   (EventNaming, EventMapBij, Orientation). It actually pulls in ELEVEN — including Space, Cover,
   Cone, ChainCone, Atlas, HDA, EventLocalSystem, and LabelSpace. The 'schedule space, atlas, cones,
   charts' that §8.1 tells you to quarantine are INSIDE the cone. Only 5 of 16 Schedule files
   (COMSheaf, Horizon, LabelChart, LocalCOM, Sculpture) are quarantinable as things stand.

BUT all 11 enter through exactly THREE ACCIDENTAL EDGES, each thin:
  a) Salvetti/BraidFunctor -> Schedule/Orientation, for TWO names: orSign, eventEquiv_id.
     This single import drags in Cover -> LabelSpace and the whole schedule-space stack.
     orSign/eventEquiv_id are EVENT-layer facts; they sit in Orientation.lean only because that is
     where w₁ happened to be developed.
  b) Salvetti/Normalize -> Schedule/Atlas, for SEVEN names: bslice, pbead, psz, psz_pos, pcubes,
     pchain, prefine. Verified: Atlas lines ~640-940 are pure CHAIN plumbing with zero references to
     Sched/Chart/Stratum/schedCone. They are misfiled — they belong in Chains/.
  c) Schedule/EventMapBij -> Schedule/Cone, for schedCone + 3 friends, used ONLY in the trailing
     corollaries (lines 375-386), AFTER eventMap_bijective (361) and eventMap_surjective (368). The
     bijectivity proof itself does not touch Cone.

ROOT CAUSE, and the thing §8.1 missed: eventEquiv is DEFINED IN Schedule/Space.lean:52 —
  eventEquiv f := Equiv.ofBijective (eventMap f) (eventMap_bijective f)
An event-layer definition parked inside the schedule-SPACE module. That is why Space (and hence the
atlas/cone/chart stack) is in the cone at all. §5 is right that eventMap_bijective is load-bearing;
it is just in the wrong folder.

THE FIX — an Events/ layer, which makes ALL of Schedule/ quarantinable (11 -> 0):
  Events/EventNaming.lean        (move; already clean — imports only Salvetti.Lines + Foundations.Altitude)
  Events/EventLocalSystem.lean   (move; already clean)
  Events/EventMapBij.lean        (move; leave the 4 cone corollaries behind in Schedule/)
  Events/EventEquiv.lean         (EXTRACT eventEquiv + eventObjLinearOrder + card_eventObj_eq_of_hom from Space.lean)
  Events/OrdSign.lean            (EXTRACT ordSign/ordPerm/ordSign_trans/orSign/eventEquiv_id from Orientation.lean)
  Chains/ChainPartition.lean     (EXTRACT the ~290-line p*/bslice block from Atlas.lean)
Then Salvetti/ imports only Events/, and the schedule space, atlas, cones, charts, LabelSpace,
Orientation's w₁, COMSheaf, Horizon, Sculpture, LabelChart, LocalCOM all quarantine cleanly.

title: DECISION: enrich over Cat, or over the braid category?
description:
USER DECIDES. Do not close this from an agent session.

HARD CONSTRAINT (§4): you CANNOT literally enrich over the braid category as hom-objects. Enrichment over V needs hom-OBJECTS in V. The hom-objects here must be groupoids — their objects are the 1-cells. The braid category's objects are strand counts. So hom-objects cannot live in Braid.

What IS true, and is the flashy statement: CFund K is a Grpd-enriched category equipped with a MONOIDAL 2-FUNCTOR to the delooped braid category, under which 1-cell composition maps to the braid TENSOR (concatenating executions adds strand counts). Both halves already exist: Flow/Flow.lean (flowHom, flowComp) and Salvetti/BraidDeloop.lean (braidDeloopComp, where composition really is n (x) m = n + m).

OPTION A — enrich in Cat (the doc's recommendation). mathlib has NO monoidal structure on Grpd (Cat has CartesianMonoidalCategory; Grpd has only Grpd.has_pi), so EnrichedCategory Grpd is not available off the shelf. Cat hom-objects Cat.of (ConcCat (K;u,v)), composition via FreeGroupoid.lift whose universal property IS strict (lift_spec is an equality). EnrichedCategory Cat = a genuine strict 2-category. Only missing input: chambersConcat associativity/unitality.

OPTION B — build the monoidal structure on Grpd first. More faithful to the intended statement, strictly more work, and mathlib will not help.

TRAP: strictness cannot pass through freeGroupoidProdEquiv — it is Localization.uniq, characterised only up to natural iso. This is what kills the naive Grpd route.

The user's §10 note asks whether we can enrich over the braid category directly (objects = naturals, morphisms n->n = braid words). Per §4 that is exactly the thing that does not typecheck as an enrichment; it IS the monoidal 2-functor. Confirm the user wants the 2-functor framing.
notes:
RESOLVED BY USER (2026-07-14). Option A: enrich in Cat. NOT Cat/B, NOT Grpd.

WHY NOT Cat/B (the slice): it is the SAME DATA as (Cat-enrichment + strict 2-functor to the delooping) — change of base along the strict monoidal forgetful U : Cat/B -> Cat is iso on the underlying 2-category. And its EXISTENCE is vacuous: the trivial lift Phi_0 (every 2-cell |-> the identity braid on its strands) is a valid Cat/B-enrichment on the same 2-category. So the slice packaging costs ~300 lines of MonoidalCategory (Over M) (absent from mathlib) to restate what we already have.

WHY NOT Grpd: mathlib gives Grpd NO monoidal structure (only HasProducts). Cat has CartesianMonoidalCategory + CatEnriched (EnrichedCategory Cat => Bicategory.Strict) off the shelf. Hom-objects are still the groupoids ConcGrpd; 'every 2-cell is invertible' is a property from FreeGroupoid, not structure the base must carry.

TARGET: CFund K : EnrichedCategory Cat, plus Phi : CFund K -> MonoidalSingleObj B, a 2-functor to the delooped braid category.

CONTENT WARNING: since Phi_0 exists, 'CFund K is braid-enriched' is VACUOUS. The theorem is that the canonical event-count grading LIFTS to a braid grading, and that OUR lift is not trivial: writhe = salWind, S_n-shadow = evPerm, loops pure iff HDA. Bead 791 (composition |-> tensor) holds for Phi_0 too and is NOT the headline.

title: READ FIRST: braid-thread landmines and refuted claims
description:
Pinned reference. Source: BRAID_ENRICHMENT_CLEANUP.md §6, §7, §8.3, §10.

LANDMINES (each cost real time):
1. equivWedgeCat / refineToWedge CARRY NonSelfLinked + AdmitsAltitude. Routing through them silently imports both while the statement LOOKS unconditional. Never route through the RefineObj<->Ch bridge when the point is to have no side conditions. RefineConcat.append is unusable for the same reason. Unconditional replacements: Chains/Segal.lean's chConcat / wedgeInclL / wedgeInclR.
2. (n :: A) ++ B vs n :: (A ++ B) are defeq but not syntactically equal, and sit inside serialWedge/pushout TYPE arguments. rw/simp/reassoc/Category.comp_id silently fail to match. Use erw with FULLY EXPLICIT arguments (bare erw triggers a whnf blow-up through pushout/colimit).
3. Type's morphisms are bundled TypeCat.Hom; comp_apply/types_comp_apply do NOT fire in simp (ConcreteCategory outParams wreck the discrimination keys). Use exact/defeq.
4. Functor.map_id / map_comp unqualified resolve to the MONAD Functor under `open CategoryTheory`. Write CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id.

REFUTED - DO NOT RESURRECT:
1. "evPerm f = 1 for every morphism <-> K is an HDA" is FALSE, machine-checked (forall_isRun_of_evPerm_one). It forces every chain to be a run, i.e. K has NO concurrency. Witness: the square has a global naming AND a non-run chain. PURITY IS A STATEMENT ABOUT LOOPS.
2. Lifting a chain loop does NOT give an execution loop. A lifted loop at `a` is a path (a, L0) ~> (a, M): same chain, DIFFERENT line. The line must be SHIFTED back (exists_lineShift). Lines must be shifted, not just lifted.

DO NOT ATTEMPT:
- Do not shorten braidFunctor. It is ~70 lines and its proof is already the right one.
- Do not reuse salSumEquiv for the braid tensor. COM.directSum's ground set is only the WITHIN-block pairs; the extension-by-block-order is the whole content.
- Do not hand-roll freeGroupoidProdEquiv. It comes free from mathlib's Localization/Prod.lean.

title: DECISION: generalize Ch/Lines/ConcCat to take endpoints, or keep repoint as the idiom?
description:
USER DECIDES. §8.2 says 'Decide once.' — so decide once.

OPTION A: generalize Ch / Lines / ConcCat to take endpoints as parameters.
OPTION B: keep BPSet.repoint K u v (Flow/ChainConcat.lean) as the official idiom and delete the bipointing from everything that does not use it.

The evidence for B: K.repoint K.init K.final = K is rfl (structure eta). That is a strong hint that repoint is the cheap, honest move and A is churn.

DONE WHEN: the choice is recorded here, with the reason.
close_reason:
DECISION: OPTION B — repoint is the idiom. Endpoints are a PARAMETER, not a commitment.

The evidence is decisive and it was already sitting in the repo:
- BPSet = <toPsh, init, final>. repoint K u v swaps only the two vertices; toPsh is untouched, so
  (K.repoint u v).cells n = K.cells n by rfl, and K.repoint K.init K.final = K by rfl (structure eta).
- Flow/ ALREADY runs entirely on this idiom: flowHom K u v := ConcGrpd (K.repoint u v), plus flowId,
  flowComp, chConcMor_assoc and the unit laws — all proved against Ch (K.repoint u v).

Option A (generalize Ch/Lines/ConcCat to take endpoints) would rebuild by hand exactly what repoint
gives for free, and would break every one of those existing lemmas. It is pure churn. Rejected.

NOTE the §8.2 premise ('BPSet is bipointed and most of the development does not need it') is
MISLEADING. The bipointing is STRUCTURAL, not spurious: Ch K is by definition the chains from K.init
to K.final, so Ch — and everything downstream (Lines, ConcCat, braidFunctor) — genuinely needs a
BPSet. What those files do NOT do is READ the endpoints: BraidFunctor, Lines, ConcGroupoid and
EventNaming contain ZERO references to K.init/K.final. They need endpoints to EXIST, not to be
anything in particular. Parameterizing them (repoint) is therefore the whole and correct answer.

title: The enrichment: build Fund, CFund, and the projection
description:
BRAID_ENRICHMENT_CLEANUP.md §3, §4. Three-tier picture, each the quotient of the last:
  1-cells = (chain, line)  2-cells = braids         ConcGrpd K   <- CFund
  1-cells = chains         2-cells = dihomotopies   Pi_1(P K)    <- Fund
  1-cells = trace classes  2-cells = trivial        pi_1(K)      <- classical

CFund -> Fund is literally 'forget the line', and it is a projection. Fullness is ALREADY PROVED (linesRestrict_surjective + exists_conc_zigzag, Salvetti/PurityHDA.lean). Kernel = the local pure braid groups.

title: Design record: computational model of the braidGrading tower (READ FIRST)
description:
The architecture and constraints every gvp bead follows. SCOPE: only the braidGrading tower (Ch K -> Lines K -> Int(Lines K) -> evKey/evPerm/event-count -> the braid category -> braidGrading). Nothing else in Testing/ or the repo.

APPROACH (B, chosen): make the REAL topos-model tower computable by starting from finite data (finite cells + face table -> PrecubicalConstructions -> Nerve -> BPSet) and giving computable bodies to the noncomputable ops. Fidelity by SOUND @[csimp] (a proof spec = impl), NOT trusted @[implemented_by].

SMALL LOCAL CORE REFACTORS are permitted and preferred: delete the .choose block calls (blockIdx/blockFace), give Chamber a decidable order, rewrite nEvents = dims-sum. Use @[csimp] only where a direct refactor would be large or destabilise a proof, notably the wedge pushout.

THE WEDGE PUSHOUT is a pointed glue: gluing two presheaves at one vertex = disjoint-union-of-cells + one identification, and desc = a case-split on the summand. Implement wedge2/serialWedge/iota/desc computably + csimp. A necessary evil for any computational model of this math.

DO NOT route the compute path through equivWedgeCat: it carries NonSelfLinked + AdmitsAltitude, which the target K will not satisfy, so it would silently narrow the domain. It stays a theorem-transport tool only.

DO NOT enumerate Ch K / ConcCat K: loops make them infinite. Evaluate the functor POINTWISE on given executions/refinements.

OUTPUT: braidGrading.map f = ofPerm (evPerm f), a single germ generator, so the computable signal is evPerm f : Perm (Fin (nEvents x)). Emit a NON-normalized Artin braid word (List of signed generator indices) from a reduced word of that permutation; writhe = permLen. Feed GAP later. The PresentedGroup word problem is never on braidGrading critical path (only braidGrpd).
design: Coq-extraction contrast that motivates the approach: Lean has no separate extraction pass; noncomputable is a static refusal; @[csimp] internalises the runnable-vs-spec equality as a kernel-checked theorem (vs Coq trusting an external erasure). So we bolt verified computable bodies onto the noncomputable spec rather than rewriting the theorems.

title: The exact sequence: forgetting the line loses only PURE braids
description:
PROVED (Braid/Purity.lean, green, sorry-free):
  permHom_braidPhi_eq_one_of_concProjN : concProjN gamma = id  =>  permHom (Phi gamma) = 1
i.e. a loop of executions whose CHAIN-zigzag is trivial has a braid with trivial permutation.
Since PureBraid n := (permHom n).ker, that IS 'its braid is pure'.

THE MAP OF EXACT SEQUENCES:
  1 -> ker(concProjN) -> Aut(mk x) --concProjN--> Aut(mk (chain x))
          |                 |                          |
          | Phi             | Phi                      | rho  (chain event monodromy)
          v                 v                          v
  1 ->  PureBraid n ----> Braid n  ---permHom------> Perm (Fin n)

 - bottom row exact BY DEFINITION (PureBraid n = ker permHom).
 - RIGHT SQUARE COMMUTES: braidPhi_comp_permHom (the permutation part of the braid IS the event
   monodromy -- germ version, new) + concRho_forget (the event monodromy reads ONLY THE CHAIN:
   concFrameSystem's action on a morphism is eventEquiv of its chain map, so it factors through
   'forget the line' -- new).
 - TOP ROW SPLIT by the standard line (stdSectionGrpd_comp_concProj, Braid/ChGrading).
 - LEFT VERTICAL = the induced map on kernels. That is this theorem.

WHY IT MATTERS: it says exactly what Ch(K) can miss. NOT arbitrary braids -- only PURE ones. The
S_n-shadow of the braid functor is a CHAIN invariant, full stop; the line buys only the lift from
S_n to B_n, and the entire discrepancy lands in P_n.

CONVERSE FAILS, and that is the point: Ch(box^n) has a terminal object, so EVERY loop is
concProjN-trivial there, yet the braids are nontrivial pure braids. On box^2 the generator of
pi_1(ConcGrpd) = Z maps to the FULL TWIST sigma_1^-2 (hand-computed; the 4-object K_2,2 graph).

ALSO LANDED: subsingleton_hom_of_isTerminal (Foundations/FreeGroupoidLift) -- the free groupoid on a
category with a terminal object is CODISCRETE. Elementary (build 1 = const(mk t) via liftNatIso from
its generator components); no nerve, no Gabriel-Zisman. This is the 'Ch(box^n) is contractible' half.

STILL OPEN: (a) Ch(serial wedge) has a terminal object -- the taut chain <dims, id>; 3 lines, not yet
written. (b) whether Phi(ker) is GENERATED by the local cube twists, i.e. whether a global Fund loop
acts nontrivially on them. That is the remaining K-dependent content.
close_reason: PROVED in Braid/Purity.lean (green, sorry-free): permHom_braidPhi_eq_one_of_concProjN' — a loop of executions whose chain-zigzag is trivial has a braid with trivial permutation, i.e. it is PURE. Plus the split exactness (stdSectionN_comp_chainProjN' = rfl; stdSectionGrpdN_comp_concProjN' = 𝟭; loopProj_surjective). Right square commutes via braidPhi_comp_permHom + concRho_forget'.

title: Cut the Salvetti dependency: permLen_evPerm_comp directly, then retire the arrangement stack
description:
GOAL: braidPsiGerm's functoriality currently routes through Salvetti (writhe is a functor, via writhe_braidPsi). That ONE lemma -- permLen_evPerm_comp (Braid/Functor.lean:46) -- is the only thing making Salvetti/BraidFunctor (550) + BraidCharacters (910) load-bearing.

LANDED THIS SESSION (green, sorry-free):
* Braid/Germ.lean: writheHom : Braid n ->* Multiplicative Z, via PresentedGroup.toGroup. EIGHT LINES.
  The germ relation IS additivity of the crossing count, so writhe falls straight out of the
  presentation. Replaces the whole writhe section of BraidCharacters (~800 ln of Sal machinery).
* Braid/Crossing.lean (NEW): the CROSSING CRITERION, Salvetti-free --
    evKey_lt_iff_of_not_split : a refinement changes the order of two events ONLY by splitting the
    bead that held them (same coarse bead, different fine beads). Every other case is forced:
      - different coarse beads -> bead order is monotone (serialWedge_blockIdx_monotone)
      - same fine bead -> the fine chamber IS the coarse one restricted (Chamber.restrict_lt, rfl)
  Plus concRef, concRef_line (THE key fact: the finer line is RESTRICTED, not chosen -- that is what
  forces the criterion), coarseBead, coarseBead_monotone.

REMAINING (the bridge to permLen):
1. DISJOINTNESS. For f : x -> y, g : y -> z: crossed(f) needs DIFFERENT y-beads (criterion), and
   crossed(g) needs the SAME y-bead (criterion applied to g). Disjoint, immediately.
2. THE COUNT. Pull all three evKey orders back to EventObj z.chain (the finest). For three linear
   orders on ONE set, crossed(x,z) = crossed(x,y) XOR crossed(y,z) -- pure Bool, no permutation
   gymnastics. With (1) the XOR is a disjoint union, so cards ADD.
3. permLen (evPerm f) = #crossed pairs -- a Finset.card_bij through evIdx/eventEquiv.
   => permLen_evPerm_comp, with no Salvetti.

THEN:
4. Move ConcN / ConcCatN / ConcGrpdN / evIdx / evPerm / fineBead OUT of Salvetti/BraidFunctor into a
   neutral file (they only need evKey, keyEquiv, EventObj, eventEquiv -- they are in the wrong home).
5. Braid/Functor.lean drops 'import CubeChains.Salvetti.BraidCharacters'.
6. Salvetti/ becomes what it should be: the GEOMETRY (Salvetti's theorem, ConcGrpd(box^n) ~= P_n) --
   an input you want, not a detour you are forced through.

ALSO PENDING (Cubical-xhj.5): braidGrading : ConcCat (K;u,v) => Braids, obj x = strands (nEvents x).
Braid/Grading.lean is already the transport layer (braidCast, nEvents, evPerm', braidHom_eqToHom);
the functor itself is not built. This is the user's stated end target.
notes:
LANDED (green, sorry-free, full lib builds):

title: The delooping: rebuild it on Braids as EnrichedCategory Cat PUnit (BraidDeloop's cannot be a strict-2-functor target)
description:
CORRECTION to the first framing of this bead: the delooping is NEEDED — it is the target of the 2-functor CFund => B(Braid) (Cubical-791). The claim is narrower and it is this:

*** BraidDeloop's delooping CANNOT serve as the target of a strict 2-functor from CFund. ***

The target of an EnrichedFunctor Cat must itself be an EnrichedCategory Cat, whose 'assoc' axiom is an EQUALITY of functors. braidDeloopComp (BraidDeloop.lean:699) is built as
    (freeGroupoidProdEquiv BraidSig BraidSig).inverse >> FreeGroupoid.map braidTensorSig
and freeGroupoidProdEquiv = Localization.uniq is pinned only UP TO NATURAL ISO. So it cannot satisfy an equality-of-functors assoc. This is the exact trap Flow/Flow.lean:387 and FreeGroupoidLift.lean:9 document, and it is why lift2 exists.

WHY THE GERM STACK DOES NOT HIT IT AT ALL:
  Salvetti: BraidCat n is the ACTION CATEGORY of S_n on the Salvetti poset — morphisms are crossings,
            NOT invertible. So you MUST groupoidify (BraidGrpdSig = FreeGroupoid BraidSig), and then
            comp is a functor out of a PRODUCT OF FREE GROUPOIDS -> lift2 dance required.
  Germ:     Braids = Sigma n, SingleObj (Braid n) is ALREADY a groupoid (Braid/Category.lean:41),
            because braids are invertible. So comp = braidsTensor is a PLAIN FUNCTOR
            Braids x Braids => Braids. The enriched assoc is a bare Functor.ext.
            NO free-groupoid machinery at all — no lift2, no lift3_ext.

THE BUILD:
  def BDeloop : Type := PUnit                      -- one 0-cell
  instance : EnrichedCategory Cat BDeloop where
    Hom _ _ := Cat.of Braids                       -- 1-cells = strand counts, 2-cells = braids
    id _    := Cat.Hom.ofFunctor (Cat.fromChosenTerminalEquiv.symm braidsUnit)
    comp    := Cat.Hom.ofFunctor braidsTensor      -- juxtaposition: n (x) m = n + m
  Three laws, each a Functor.ext whose h_obj is congrArg strands (Nat.add_assoc / add_zero / zero_add)
  and whose h_map is the juxHom coherence.

MISSING INPUTS (this is the whole job): Braid/Category.lean has braidsTensor_obj, braidsTensor_map,
braidsUnit — but NO assoc/unit lemmas. Need the braidsTensor_* obj+map pair, same two-column shape as
concConc_obj_* / concConc_map_* in Flow/CFund.lean.

NOT MonoidalSingleObj: it needs MonoidalCategory Braids (associator/unitor isos + pentagon + triangle)
and yields a NON-strict Bicategory, so a 2-functor out of the strict CFund would owe coherence. The
enriched route skips all of it.

THEN Cubical-791 is EnrichedFunctor Cat (CFund K) BDeloop — mathlib's EnrichedFunctor (Enriched/Basic.lean:288),
two laws: map_id and map_comp. map_comp IS the flashy statement: concatenation of executions |-> juxtaposition
of braids, so strand counts ADD.

ONLY THEN does BraidDeloop.lean become retirable (it is already an orphan: nothing imports it; the sole
reference is a comment at Braid/Category.lean:15).
notes:
ALGEBRAIC CORE DONE (green, sorry-free, committed):
* Braid/JuxAssoc.lean: juxHom_assoc (braidCast(add_assoc)(juxHom(n+m)k(juxHom n m(a,b),c)) = juxHom n(m+k)(a,juxHom m k(b,c))), juxHom_zero_left/right. Via blockPerm_assoc on ofPerm generators + three block-slides. Also braidHom_ext, braidCastHom.
* Braid/Deloop.lean: braidsTensor_obj_assoc/_id_left/_id_right (obj level), braidsTensor_map_assoc (in braidHom form), and the helpers braidCast_braidCast, strands_inj, eqToHom_braidHom_eqToHom.

REMAINING: just the EnrichedCategory Cat PUnit instance itself. The three axioms peel (as in Flow/CFund) to Functor.ext over braidsTensor, whose h_obj halves ARE braidsTensor_obj_* and whose h_map halves ARE braidsTensor_map_*. The one obstacle: the eqToHom from Functor.ext's h_obj has implicit object args 'braidsTensor.obj (strands.., strands..)' while the braidHom-slide lemmas expect 'strands (..)' — DEFEQ but not syntactic, so rw won't match and simp-normalizing doesn't fix the proof term inside eqToHom.

CLEAN ROUTE (next session): prove the map axiom via HEq instead of eqToHom-matching. Use eqToHom_comp_heq_iff / comp_eqToHom_heq_iff (mathlib) to reduce 'braidHom u = eqToHom p ≫ braidHom v ≫ eqToHom p'.symm' to 'HEq (braidHom u) (braidHom v)', then to 'HEq u v' at the braid level, discharged by juxHom_assoc (u = braidCast(add_assoc).symm v, and braidCast is a cast so HEq u v). This sidesteps all proof-term matching.

THEN 791 (composition |-> braid tensor) is EnrichedFunctor Cat CFund deloop, map = braidGrpd, map_comp = 'concatenating executions juxtaposes braids'.

Conceptual reason: an EnrichedFunctor to the delooping IS just 'braidGrpd on each hom-object, compatibly with composition' — the delooping adds no content, only packaging, and its one-object target collapses the 1-cell structure. The only real statement (composition ↦ braid tensor) is a single functor equation braidGrpd(concat) = braidsTensor(braidGrpd, braidGrpd), stateable without the enriched instance — but the user is deferring even that. The whole braid-tensor cluster (Salvetti/BraidDeloop, Braid/Deloop, Braid/JuxAssoc, Braid/Jux, Braid/BlockPerm, braidsTensor) is being removed as dead. juxHom_assoc, blockPerm, etc. are ~20-line rebuilds if ever wanted.

title: the braid functor sends cubes to pure braids
description:
The goal is to prove that `braidGrading` sends `ConcCat(cube n)` to the pure braids as an isomorphism.

There are three features to that: always pure, surjective, and injective. Some of these are harder than others, but all three are required for this to be done.

A few things to note. We have, via the salvetti machinery that already exists, isomorphisms between ConcCat(n) and the pure braid group. (perhaps you need an axiom there, but I can accept an axiom for Salvetti's theorem. If it becomes critical, stop, and make a bead forcing a decision). However, be aware that this is not enough. We need to see that `braidGrading` behaves nicely. Only use the salvetti stuff if it's a big proof simplification.
notes:
NICE STATE + KEY CLARIFICATIONS (2026-07-15, all files green sorry-free):

π₁-vs-SALVETTI (important): ConcBraid(□n) x IS π₁(ConcCat(□n)) by definition, so the target ConcBraid≅Pₙ literally IS 'π₁(concurrency)=pure braid group' — the π₁ SHADOW of Salvetti's theorem. But NOT the full theorem: full Salvetti = homotopy equivalence |Sal|≃complement (all π_i); we prove ONLY π₁ via van Kampen (2-skeleton), never touch π_{≥2}/asphericity. π₁ alone is the accessible Artin/Randell-presentation computation. The content concentrates in GENERATION (every concurrency morphism = product of elementary braidings) — the hard heart, a real bounded theorem, NOT a freebie (earlier 'clean' was too glib). Real proof, no hidden axiom.

Φ̃ CONFIRMED OBSTRUCTED: no hom LabConc→Braid n extending braidGrpd — relabeling conjugates evPerm', but permLen (Coxeter length) is NOT conjugation-invariant, so ofPerm ρ ↦ ofPerm(σρσ⁻¹) breaks the germ relations. ⟹ the square-bead/triangle holonomy loop is needed under EVERY route (not redundant with the LabConc route).

SURJECTIVITY (Braid/Surjectivity.lean, green):
- STEP 2/3 DONE sorry-free: Schreier spine (pureBraid_le_of_schreier, concPureBraidHom_surjective_of_realizes, closure_range_ofPerm), transports, x₀, keyEquiv_surjective, braidGrpd_transport + braidGrpd_surjective_onBraid (reusable full-braid-target lemmas).
- STEP 1 (the adjT-j holonomy loop) is WRITTEN as a COMPLETE proof (concPureBraidHom_surjective via a nice TRIANGLE-LOOP: 3 lines of the single cube x₀ sharing one chain = a genuine loop, NO cross-chain matching — cleaner than the square-bead I'd specced) but FENCED in a /- -/ block: it grinds at maxHeartbeats 4000000 (whnf blowup unfolding braidGrpd.obj through FreeGroupoid.lift). PERF-FIX = route the braid read-off through readBraid morphism-equation form (never force endBraid on a raw braidGrpd.map), then unfence. This is the top of the file's RESTART NOTE too.

NEXT: (a) perf-fix + unfence → concPureBraidHom_surjective green (surjectivity closed). (b) INJECTIVITY: LabConc (strict Sal side) + Ψ:Braid n→LabConc (toGroup, germ relations banked) + section concBraidHom∘Ψ|_Pₙ=id (needs holonomy) + GENERATION ⟹ concBraidHom bijective, delete SalvettiFaithful. Consolidation of the two tracks DEFERRED (user: real proof first). All axiom-free, no topology, no Matsumoto.

Gate done: Salvetti/BraidReindex.lean (Sₙ-action via reorient+braidSalEquiv).

title: BRAID_ENRICHMENT.md: rewrite §1 and §4 — both claims are wrong
description:
Two claims in the doc are false as written.

§1/§4 'CFund K = ->pi_1(K) with every hom-set replaced by a BRAID GROUP' -- FALSE twice over. Hom-objects are GROUPOIDS, not groups; and their vertex groups are PURE and REDUCIBLE: a single n-cube gives P_n (concCubeEquiv + Salvetti), and a wedge gives prod_i P_{d_i} (concWedgeSerialEquiv), i.e. the block-diagonal pure subgroup of B_n. The full braid group is never a vertex group -- non-pure braids arise ONLY from event monodromy, which is global. The sentence only reads as true because 'concurrency braid group' is currently DEFINED to be the vertex group, making it circular.

§4 'you cannot enrich over the braid category' -- true but weak. The real statement is a DEGENERACY THEOREM: a B-enriched category is exactly a SET. Unit forces l(x,x)=0; composition forces l(x,y)+l(y,z)=l(x,z); take z:=x and l >= 0 in N, so l == 0. Nothing to do with K. Worth ~20 lines of Lean.

REPLACEMENT FRAMING: CFund K carries a canonical BRAID GRADING Phi (a lift of the forced event-count grading through B ->> N). Existence of SOME lift is vacuous (the trivial lift Phi_0 sending every 2-cell to the identity braid works), so the theorem is that OUR lift is geometric and non-trivial: writhe = salWind, S_n-shadow = evPerm, loops pure iff HDA, an n-cube contributes P_n, beads in series contribute the tensor.

Also: the braid group is now defined by its GARSIDE GERM (Braid/Germ.lean) -- one generator per permutation, relation [s][t]=[st] when lengths add. The Artin relations are CONSEQUENCES (the braid relation is the 3-cube: two length-additive factorisations of the same A_2 longest element). Salvetti is no longer needed to call the target 'braids'; what is cited instead is Matsumoto/Garside (pure algebra), and only for identifying the germ presentation with the Artin one.
close_reason: BRAID_ENRICHMENT.md §1 and §4 rewritten. Killed both false claims: (a) 'CFund = pi_1(K) with every hom-set replaced by a BRAID GROUP' — hom-objects are GROUPOIDS not groups, and their vertex groups are PURE and REDUCIBLE (P_n for a cube, prod_i P_{d_i} for a wedge); the full braid group is never a cube's vertex group. (b) 'CFund is Grpd-enriched' — it is Cat-enriched (mathlib gives Grpd no monoidal structure). §4 now carries the DEGENERACY THEOREM for B-enrichment (unit forces l(x,x)=0, composition forces additivity, z:=x and l>=0 in N give l==0, so a B-enriched category is just a set) and the correct positive statement: CFund is Cat-enriched (hence Bicategory.Strict via CatEnriched) and carries the braid grading braidGrading : Int(Lines K) => B. Also flagged that BraidDeloop cannot be the tensor target (Localization.uniq), and added the purity result to §1's invariants — plus an explicit warning not to say 'ker Phi is the branching ambiguity' (the proved statement is about ker of the PROJECTION, and Phi does not factor through it).

title: Global grading: ConcCat K => Braids (all strand counts at once)
description:
GAP FOUND: the repo has NEVER had a globally-graded braid functor. Salvetti/BraidDeloop only has braidGrpdIncl (n) : BraidGrpd n => BraidGrpdSig, one stratum at a time. So the doc's 'monoidal 2-functor to the delooped braid category' was never actually assembled -- Phi existed only per-n.

Needed because composition CHANGES the strand count (n (x) m = n+m), so the 2-functor cannot be stated per-stratum.

WHY IT IS WORK, NOT BOOKKEEPING. ConcCat K is not literally a Sigma, so obj x = strands (nEvents x) and obj y = strands (nEvents y) differ syntactically while nEvents x = nEvents y only PROPOSITIONALLY (card_eventObj_eq_of_hom). SigmaHom.mk demands definitional index equality, so map f needs an eqToHom:

  map f := braidHom (ofPerm (evPermAll f)) >> eqToHom (congrArg strands (card_eventObj_eq_of_hom f))

and map_comp then has to push eqToHom past braidHom. Two supporting lemmas:
  (1) eqToHom_braidHom: eqToHom h >> braidHom b = braidHom (cast h b) >> eqToHom h  (by subst)
  (2) evPerm coherence across strata: evPerm computed in stratum nx vs ny is related by finCongr -- evPerm goes through evIdx, which uses finCongr x.property, so this needs proving.

Estimate ~100-200 lines of dependent-type work. Blocks the 2-functor and the enrichment.
notes:
STARTED. Braid/Grading.lean builds (green, axiom-clean) with the transport layer done:

  braidCast h b            -- transport a braid along n = m
  braidCast_ofPerm         -- braidCast h (ofPerm s) = ofPerm (permCongr (finCongr h) s)
  permLen_permCongr        -- length is recast-invariant
  braidHom_eqToHom         -- a recast slides past a braid  (by subst)
  nEvents x                -- Fintype.card (EventObj x.chain)
  concRefine' f            -- f.1.unop, at the ConcCat level (no stratum)
  nEvents_eq f             -- card_eventObj_eq_of_hom: the strand count is well defined
  evIdx' x                 -- keyEquiv (evKey x.line): NO finCongr needed, since
                              nEvents x is DEFINITIONALLY Fintype.card (EventObj x.chain)
  evPerm' f                -- the event permutation, read in the SOURCE's frame

REMAINING (3 lemmas + the functor):
  1. evPerm'_id  : evPerm' (id x) = 1
  2. evPerm'_comp: evPerm' (f >> g)
       = (finCongr (nEvents_eq f).symm).permCongr (evPerm' g) * evPerm' f
     (mirror evPerm_comp; needs eventEquiv_comp)
  3. permLen additivity at ConcCat level. Easiest route: prove
       evPerm' f = evPerm (x := <x, rfl>) (y := <y, (nEvents_eq f).symm>) f
     -- should be near-rfl, because x.property is literally rfl and
     finCongr rfl = refl -- then reuse permLen_evPerm_comp + permLen_permCongr.
     GOTCHA: ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory homs are a STRUCTURE with a .hom field
     (see concRefine in BraidFunctor.lean:354, which writes f.hom.1.unop), so f must
     be wrapped, not passed directly.
  4. braidGrading : ConcCat K => Braids
       obj x := strands (nEvents x)
       map f := braidHom (ofPerm (evPerm' f)) >> eqToHom (congrArg strands (nEvents_eq f))
     map_id  : eqToHom_refl accepts ANY proof of X = X, and proof irrelevance makes
               <x, nEvents_eq (id x)> defeq to <x, rfl>.
     map_comp: push eqToHom past braidHom with braidHom_eqToHom, then it is (2) + (3).
close_reason: braidGrading : ConcCat K ⥤ Braids and braidGrpd : ConcGrpd K ⥤ Braids landed in Braid/Grading.lean, sorry-free. obj x = strands (nEvents x); map f = braidHom (ofPerm (evPerm' f)) ≫ eqToHom recast. Verified by rfl.

title: Phi : ConcGrpd K -> B, the honest braid functor
description:
Psi f := ofPerm (evPerm f); Phi := FreeGroupoid.lift Psi.

Functoriality is ~20 lines from lemmas that already exist: writhe is a FUNCTOR (BraidCharacters.lean:822) and writhe_braidPsi (:887) says writhe = invCount of evPerm, so invCount ADDS along composable refinements -- which is exactly the germ relation. Needs a bridge lemma permLen = invCount (BraidCharacters.lean:859 defines invCount over BraidGround).

Then permHom . Phi = evPerm (Phi lifts the event monodromy) and writhe . Phi = salWind.
close_reason: Braid/Functor.lean builds, axiom-clean. invCount_eq_permLen (bridge), permLen_evPerm_comp (THE germ relation, derived from writhe being a functor + writhe_braidPsi), braidPsiGerm : ConcCatN K n => BraidFib n (f |-> ofPerm (evPerm f)), braidPhi := FreeGroupoid.lift, permHom_braidPhi (Phi lifts the event monodromy). Per-stratum; global grading split out.

title: Braid/Category.lean — the delooped braid category B
description:
B := Sigma n, SingleObj (Braid n): objects are strand counts (NO data on objects, unlike BraidSig), Hom(n,n) = Braid n, tensor n (x) m = n+m by juxtaposition.

Juxtaposition hom Braid n x Braid m ->* Braid (n+m) comes from blockPerm: needs permLen (blockPerm s t) = permLen s + permLen t (cross-block pairs are never inverted -- easy). blockPerm_one/_mul/_assoc are ALREADY PROVED in Salvetti/BraidDeloop.lean:185-200,673 -- extract them to a low-level Braid/BlockPerm.lean so Braid/ does not depend on the Salvetti stack, and have BraidDeloop import it.

Strictness is cheap here precisely BECAUSE objects are naturals: assoc is Nat.add_assoc, not an HEq over Salvetti cells. This is the payoff for dropping BraidSig.
close_reason: Braid/{BlockPerm,Jux,Category}.lean all build clean. blockPerm extracted from Salvetti/BraidDeloop (no proof changes); permLen_blockPerm proved; juxHom via MonoidHom.noncommCoprod; Braids = Sigma n, SingleObj (Braid n) with Groupoid instance; braidsTensor + braidsTensor_map (composition |-> juxtaposition).

title: Braid/Germ.lean — the braid group by its Garside germ
description: Braid n := PresentedGroup over Perm (Fin n), one generator per permutation, relation [sigma][tau] = [sigma tau] when permLen adds. permHom : Braid n ->* Perm (Fin n); PureBraid n := permHom.ker. Mathlib-only, upstreamable.
close_reason: Built and building clean; axiom-clean (propext/choice/Quot.sound only). Braid n, permLen, germRels, ofPerm, ofPerm_mul, ofPerm_one, permHom, permHom_surjective, PureBraid.

title: Build CFund K — the complexified fundamental category
description:
§1, §2.1: 0-cells = vertices; 1-cells = COMPLEXIFIED chains (= chain + line); 2-cells = braids.

  Lines K : (Ch K)^op => Type     a chain |-> prod_i (a chamber = a total order of bead i's directions)
  ConcCat K := (Lines K).Elements  objects = (chain a, line L)   <- THE 1-CELLS
  ConcGrpd K := FreeGroupoid (ConcCat K)                          <- THE 2-CELLS

THE LINE IS THE COMPLEXIFICATION: a chain is a FACE of the local braid arrangement (which events are simultaneous); a line is a TOPE above it (in which order the simultaneous ones are taken). That pair is exactly a Salvetti cell. This is the sentence to lead the paper with.

DONE WHEN: CFund K is defined and its laws hold, sorry-free.
close_reason: CFund K built: def CFund + EnrichedCategory Cat (CFund K) in Flow/CFund.lean, sorry-free. Bicategory.Strict (CatEnriched (CFund K)) derives by inferInstance.

title: The projection CFund ->> Fund (forget the line)
description:
§3. Assembly — the hard part is already proved.

  CategoryOfElements.pi (Lines K) : ConcCat K => (Ch K)^op
  FreeGroupoid.map (...)          : ConcGrpd K => FreeGroupoid ((Ch K)^op) = Pi_1(P K)   [Gabriel-Zisman]

- Surjective on 1-cells: every chain has at least one line.
- FULL on 2-cells: ALREADY PROVED — linesRestrict_surjective + exists_conc_zigzag (Salvetti/PurityHDA.lean). Every zigzag of chains lifts to a zigzag of executions.
- Kernel = the local pure braid groups.

This is the precise sense in which the result generalizes existing directed-topology work: Fund is the real picture, CFund its complexification, and the braid content is exactly what the projection destroys.

DONE WHEN: a full surjection of enriched categories, identity on 0-cells, sorry-free.
notes:
NOW UNBLOCKED — both ends exist.
  CFund : EnrichedCategory Cat (CFund K)   hom u v = ConcGrpd (K.repoint u v)   (Flow/CFund.lean)
  Fund  : EnrichedCategory Cat (Fund K)    hom u v = FreeGroupoid ((Ch (K.repoint u v))ᵒᵖ)  (Flow/Fund.lean)
and the hom-level projection is ALREADY BUILT: concProj K = FreeGroupoid.map (CategoryOfElements.π (Lines K)) : ConcGrpd K ⥤ FreeGroupoid ((Ch K)ᵒᵖ)  (Braid/ChGrading.lean).

SO 4i3 = make it an EnrichedFunctor Cat (CFund K) (Fund K):
  obj  := id on vertices
  map u v := Cat.Hom.ofFunctor (concProj (K.repoint u v))
  map_id  : concProj sends the identity execution to the identity chain — should be rfl/lift_ext.
  map_comp: concProj (concGrpdConc a b) = fundConc (concProj a) (concProj b), i.e. FORGETTING THE LINE
            COMMUTES WITH CONCATENATION. On generators this is π (concConc (x,y)) = chConcatOp (π x, π y),
            which is essentially rfl (concConc's chain part IS chConc). Then lift₂_ext carries it.

ALSO FREE, and worth stating: the SECTION. stdSection : (Ch K)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ConcCat K is a strict section of π
(Braid/ChGrading, stdSection_comp_π = rfl), so if it is monoidal for concatenation — i.e. the standard
line of a concatenation is the concatenation of the standard lines, which should be immediate since
stdLine reads only dims — then Fund => CFund is an EnrichedFunctor SPLITTING the projection. That would
make CFund ->> Fund a SPLIT epi of 2-categories, upgrading the hom-groupoid splitting already proved in
Braid/Purity (stdSectionGrpdN_comp_concProjN').
close_reason: Flow/Project.lean landed, green, sorry-free. cfundToFund K : EnrichedFunctor Cat (CFund K) (Fund K) — identity on vertices, flowProj = FreeGroupoid.map (CategoryOfElements.π (Lines ...)) on hom-objects. map_comp IS the content (concGrpdConc_comp_flowProj: forgetting the line commutes with concatenation), and on generators it is concConc_comp_π := rfl — because the chain half of concConc IS chConc. lift₂_ext carries it to the groupoids.

title: The monoidal 2-functor CFund -> B(Braid): composition |-> tensor
description:
§4, the flashy statement. Concatenating executions ADDS strand counts, so 1-cell composition maps to the braid tensor n (x) m = n + m.

Both halves exist: Flow/Flow.lean (flowHom, flowComp) and Salvetti/BraidDeloop.lean (braidDeloopComp). This ticket wires them into an actual monoidal 2-functor.

DONE WHEN: the 2-functor is defined with its coherence, sorry-free.
close_reason: WON'T BUILD — user decision (2026-07-14), same as ndf.3. The monoidal 2-functor CFund → B(Braid) is dropped. If ever wanted, it is NOT a separate construction: it is braidGrpd assembled across hom-objects, and its only content is the equation braidGrpd(concGrpdConc x y) = braidsTensor(braidGrpd x, braidGrpd y), which reduces on generators to 'the event permutation of a concatenation is the block permutation of the two'. No delooping-as-enriched-category needed.

title: End-to-end braid-word extraction on a concrete K + axiom gate
description: Wire the tower into a top-level computable braidWordOf on a small concrete K (a square, then an n=3 example where braiding first appears). #eval it and @[export] the entry point. GATE: #print axioms braidWordOf must show no Classical.choice on the computation path (correctness proofs may still use it). Produces the runnable artifact and a GAP-pasteable braid word.
design: HARNESS GOAL (user, this session): a reusable test harness — define K, run braidGrading, get braid words back. STATUS: the compute CORE is done and #eval-able (permWord/braidWord/wordZToBraid/invWordZ/schreierWordZ in Braid/PermWord.lean; braidWord f = permWordZ (evPerm' f)). title: Extracting implementations
description:
We cannot test our code on small examples very easily because native_decide is willing to tolerate all the `Set` and noncomputables we have. On the other hand, all the noncomputables are spurious, arising from various generalized constructions (like pushouts) that are actually quite computable in our setting. The goals of this epic are to 
1. Testing hypothesis against real examples
2. Extract implementations for integrating it into, say, a haskell library

Failure modes are 
1. duplicating implementations
2. losing generality of the theorems

I don't care about performance. If the extracted thing is slow that's fine. I also do not care about legibility. As long as we are extracting "exactly" what we are proving stuff about, it does not need to be nice.

title: xhj.6 general-n: forced axiom decision (Pₙ has no presentation; abstract Salvetti iso insufficient)
description:
Deciding how to close features 2+3 (injectivity + surjectivity) of xhj.6 for general n. Feature 1 (purity) is DONE on the real functor (cube_braidGrpd_pure, CubeChains/Braid/CubeIso.lean). The remaining iso ConcBraid(□n) x ≅ Pₙ = ker(permHom n) forces a decision, per subagent findings:

1. Pₙ HAS NO PRESENTATION anywhere (mathlib or repo) — it is only defined as ker(permHom n). Constructing the inverse map Pₙ → ConcBraid is BLOCKED without first formalizing the Artin pure-braid presentation (generators A_ij) AND proving it equals the kernel — a Reidemeister–Schreier theorem, a major undertaking.

2. AN ABSTRACT SALVETTI ISO AXIOM IS PROVABLY INSUFFICIENT. Assuming only 'ConcBraid(□n) x ≃* Pₙ' abstractly does NOT make the SPECIFIC map Φ = mapVertexGroup(braidGrpd) bijective (an abstract iso between two groups says nothing about a given homomorphism between them). Therefore any general-n axiom MUST NAME braidGrpd — e.g. 'Function.Bijective Φ', or a section s : Pₙ → ConcBraid with Φ∘s = id plus surjectivity on a generating family. This mirrors the Matsumoto-naming pattern already used in Braid/Artin.lean. THIS IS THE FORCED DECISION the parent bead anticipated.

3. SURJECTIVITY IS THE LINCHPIN. Given surjectivity of Φ onto Pₙ plus Pₙ Hopfian (finitely generated + residually finite ⟹ Hopfian; true, but not in mathlib), a surjective endomorphism of Pₙ is injective, so INJECTIVITY FOLLOWS. The converse (inj ⟹ surj) does NOT hold. Prioritize surjectivity.

4. MISSING PREREQUISITE: IsConnected(ConcGrpd(□n)) is needed and absent (Testing/ConcSpace has only native_decide b0=1 evidence). Sal(braidCOM n) has no initial/terminal/weakly-terminal object, so the xhj.11 μ-generator trick does not transfer — needs a general spanning tree + IsConnected.

RECOMMENDATION: (a) land n=2 unconditionally first (P₂=ℤ, ConcCat(□²)=K22 4-cycle, Φ via winding/writhe abelianization — no axiom needed); (b) for general n, prove surjectivity as far as possible, build IsConnected(ConcGrpd(□n)); (c) then adopt an axiom that NAMES braidGrpd (bijectivity or a named section), not an abstract Salvetti iso. DECIDE which of these before writing more general-n code.

title: ZP: pi_1(N(Ch Z)) = B_n for Z terminal — SKELETON + why it is NOT easy
description:
*** READ THIS BEFORE ATTEMPTING. THIS WAS TRIED AND ABANDONED. ***

CubeChains/FinalPrecubical/ was EXACTLY this theorem. Quarantined (ARCHITECTURE.md:36) and then deleted
('salvage old code', 24b76fc). Its STATUS.md (git show 88f2ce0) records what HAD landed green:
  * Ch(box^m) equiv OSPcat m           (ordered-partition poset)
  * Sal0Br n equiv Composition n x Perm (CompositionSalvetti, 672e4d3)
  * HomAnalysis (PARTIAL): blockMap(+monotone), blockMap_spec, fibreShape  (d8cba4e)
and it STALLED on the 'R2 crux' = the object-level Segal decomposition of a wedge map, with an explicit
note that the net size win was 'modest, not dramatic'. Salvage from git before rebuilding.

MY SKELETON (derived independently this session; DIFFERENT attack, and better-positioned now):
Let Z = terminal BPSet. r = [1,1,...,1], c = [n].
 1. Ch(Z) = THE WEDGE CATEGORY. Z terminal => the classifying map is unique and the triangle
    (phi >> b.map = a.map) is AUTOMATIC. So Hom_Ch(Z)(A,B) = ALL bipointed wedge maps <A -> <B.
    (Contrast box^n, where b.map is an iso so phi is FORCED — that is why Ch(box^n) has a terminal
    object and Ch(Z) does not.)
 2. Hom(r,c) = S_n; r weakly initial, c weakly terminal.
 3. Aut(mk r) = < mu_pi : pi in S_n >, mu_pi := g_pi g_1^-1.  EASY: for any e : A -> B pick f : B -> c
    and insert f f^-1, giving gamma_e = mu_pi mu_rho^-1. No topology, no nerve.
 4. chBraid(mu_pi) = ofPerm(pi^{+-1})  =>  chBraid : Aut(mk r) ->> B_n is SURJECTIVE. EASY.
 5. INJECTIVITY = THE THEOREM. Need Psi : B_n -> Aut(mk r), [pi] |-> mu_pi, well-defined, i.e. the germ
    relation mu_sigma mu_tau = mu_{tau sigma} for LENGTH-ADDITIVE pairs. Hand-verified at n=3: these DO
    fall out of the factorization relations g_{h>>f} = h^ >> f^, and they are exactly the PARABOLIC
    decompositions sigma = w.u (w a minimal coset rep, u in the Young subgroup S_X) — which are
    length-additive. Needs: (a) the parabolic relations GENERATE the germ presentation; (b) upstream,
    the WEDGE-MAP CLASSIFICATION (Hom(<A,<B) determined by blockIdx + blockFace, every compatible pair
    realized) — which IS HomAnalysis, i.e. exactly where the old effort stalled.

WHY BETTER-POSITIONED NOW: Braid/Germ.lean presents B_n BY THE GARSIDE GERM, so step 5's target is
literally 'check the germ relations in Aut(mk r)' — a PresentedGroup.toGroup — not a Salvetti/Segal
argument. And chBraid (Braid/ChGrading.lean) already exists.

TRACTABLE HALF (do this first if resumed): steps 1-4 => 'B_n is a quotient of pi_1 N(Ch Z)'. Bounded.

WHY IT MATTERS (the conceptual payoff, already established this session):
  box^n : H = pi_1 N(Ch) = 1 (terminal chain => codiscrete);  G = pi_1 ConcGrpd = P_n
  Z     : H = B_n (!);                                        G strictly BIGGER (F_2 at n=2)
So the two extremes are OPPOSITE. For box^n the chain category sees NOTHING and the lines carry
everything. For Z the chain category already sees the FULL braid group and adding lines OVERSHOOTS.
Reason: Z's events are UNLABELED (one cell per dimension), so refining a square has TWO parallel arrows
— and that IS the braid generator. box^n's cubes are rigid/labelled, so those parallel arrows split into
distinct objects and the category collapses. Matches 1 -> P_n -> B_n -> S_n -> 1: box^n has global event
naming (monodromy trivial, get P_n); Z has none (monodromy all of S_n, get B_n).

n=2 CHECK (hand-done): Ch(Z)_2 = 2 objects, 2 PARALLEL arrows => circle => pi_1 = Z = B_2. And
ConcCat(Z)_2 = 3 objects, 4 morphisms => b1 = 2 => G = F_2. So q : G -> H is a SPLIT SURJECTION but NOT
an iso, and Phi : F_2 ->> Z is not either. The generator of ker q maps to sigma_1^2, the full twist —
exactly as Cubical-xhj.10 predicts.

title: Linking-number characters ℓ_ij — the pure-braid invariant writhe throws away
description:
writhe is the abelianization of B_n: ONE integer. P_n abelianizes to Z^(n(n-1)/2), the pairwise LINKING NUMBERS l_ij = how many times event i winds around event j along a loop of executions. writhe = sum of l_ij, so it sums away exactly the structure we want. This is the hole in BraidCharacters, and likely why salWind has no bead-size formula (Cubical-l3v): it is a projection of a richer object.

STRUCTURE (the thing to get right): l is NOT a homomorphism on B_n -- the crossing pairs get relabelled by the permutation. It is a 1-COCYCLE
    l : B_n -> Z^Pairs(n),   l(gh) = l(g) + perm(g) . l(h)
for the S_n action permuting the pairs. Restricted to P_n (action trivial) a crossed hom IS a hom -- and that hom is the abelianization of P_n. So l_ij is exactly the braid content invisible to permutations.

The germ presentation makes this cheap: one generator [sigma] per permutation, l([sigma]) := inversions(sigma) as an element of Z^Pairs (the repo already has 'inversions' in Braid/Germ.lean). The cocycle law on a length-additive product is Inv(st) = Inv(s) union s.Inv(t) -- which is exactly salCross_add, already the composition law of refinements.

DONE WHEN: l_ij defined, cocycle law proved, restriction to pure loops is a homomorphism, and writhe = sum l_ij.
notes:
LANDED (Salvetti/Braiding.lean, green, sorry-free):
  salCrossVec a b : E -> Z          -- WHICH walls separate a from b, not how many
  salCrossVec_add                    -- additive WALL BY WALL along a <= b <= c
  salWindVec : Sal L => SingleObj (Multiplicative (E -> Z))   -- the vector winding functor
  sumWalls, sumWalls_salWindVec_map  -- salWind IS its sum
The pointwise additivity was already hiding inside salCross_add's proof: 'tope_eq_of_cross' says a wall
crossed by a<=b is not crossed again by b<=c. So the linking numbers were one un-summing away. For the
braid arrangement a WALL IS A PAIR OF EVENTS, so salWindVec is literally l_ij, and writhe = sum l_ij.

*** THE EXPERIMENT CANNOT RUN ON THE TRINITY, FOR A STRUCTURAL REASON. ***

BraidGround n = {(i,j) : i<j}, card = n(n-1)/2. MACHINE-CHECKED: card (BraidGround 2) = 1.

Every chain of the trinity runs a->d in exactly TWO events (three length-2 lines; three single 2-beads).
So n=2. At n=2 there is EXACTLY ONE WALL: the linking vector has one component and l == writhe,
identically. The refinement is VACUOUS there.

The trinity was never the right shape anyway: it IS the event-naming counterexample, so its permutation
monodromy is nontrivial BY CONSTRUCTION. It is the case where permutations already see everything.

TwoSquares has the right SHAPE (S_no_fold => global event naming => BraidPure => permutation trivial;
N(Ch S) ~= S^1 => pi_1(Fund S) = Z, a genuine global loop) but its chains are ALSO 2 events. And there is
a decisive argument it can never work: at n=2, P_2 = Z, and the single square's full twist (writhe +-2)
GENERATES ALL OF IT. So a global loop's pure braid is necessarily a multiple of the local twist.
Nothing beyond local, provably.

=> EVERY EXISTING WITNESS IN THE REPO IS BLIND TO THIS QUESTION BY CONSTRUCTION. All are built from
length-2 chains. The complexification CANNOT show content at n=2 -- not 'hasn't been shown', cannot.

SPEC FOR A WITNESS THAT COULD (now precise):
  (i)   chains with >= 3 events (>= 3 walls, so l is strictly finer than writhe);
  (ii)  global event naming / BraidPure (permutation identically trivial, braid pure) -- else
        permutations already win;
  (iii) a nontrivial loop in Fund: N(Ch K) not simply connected;
  (iv)  PAYOFF: the pure braid of that global loop is NOT in the subgroup generated by the local cubes'
        pure braids.
threeSquare / fourSquare have 4-event chains (two 2-beads in series -> 4 events -> 6 walls), so they clear
(i). (ii) and (iii) are UNCHECKED for them.

BLOCKER for any computation: Testing/Model.lean is DECOUPLED from the main library -- it models Ch K
combinatorially and knows nothing about Lines, chambers or braids. Computing l on a witness means building
the execution/chamber layer in the surrogate (chains -> chamber per bead -> refinement block data ->
crossing vectors -> cycle in the graph). Real work; worth doing ONCE, against a witness that can show
something.

title: Phi is the universal crossing invariant
description:
THE headline theorem, better than the enrichment.

STATEMENT. For any group G, an invariant of refinements that depends only on the event permutation (Psi f = phi (evPerm f)) factors UNIQUELY through Phi. So B_n is not a chosen target -- it is the FREE RECEPTACLE for crossing data. FreeGroupoid.functorEquiv (a strict bijection) reduces functors out of ConcGrpd to functors out of ConcCat, so this is a statement about refinements only.

PROOF SHAPE.
1. Functoriality of Psi forces phi (tau*sigma) = phi tau * phi sigma for exactly the pairs REALIZED by composable refinements in K.
2. SURPRISE, verified by hand: realized pairs are a PROPER SUBSET of the length-additive pairs. Counterexample in cube^3: F_x = 1<2<3, F_y = 2<1<3, F_z = 3<2<1. sigma = flip(1,2), tau = flip(1,3),(2,3); lengths add (3 = 1+2) but tau forces 1,2,3 into one bead of y, so by serialWedge_blockIdx_monotone 1,2 share a bead of x, so sigma cannot flip them. NOT realizable.
3. It works anyway because the realized pairs GENERATE: every atom relation [sigma][s] = [sigma s] (s an adjacent transposition, lengths adding) IS realized -- s flips two F_y-adjacent events which sigma did not flip (no double crossing), so {e,f} is a legal bead. Atoms generate the germ (Garside).
4. Then PresentedGroup.toGroup gives the unique extension.

CAVEAT: this is a statement about K containing enough cube. For K a graph there are no refinements and the receptacle is trivial -- correctly.

CONFIDENCE: ~85%. The two load-bearing steps are the atom-realization (checked by hand, step 3) and 'atoms generate the germ' (classical Garside, needs a real citation or a proof).

DEPENDS ON: Phi existing at all -- cannot even be stated first.

⚠ THE HARD PART, AND THE REFUTED ONE. The local matchings DO NOT GLUE, and a time function does not fix it (8-cycle on fourSquare). MORSE.md has a corrected '(M, corrected)' box — read it, and read its caveat about the trinity. Do not start from §4(a).

title: L9: Sched K = colim C ≃ hocolim C ≃ |N(Ch K)|
description:
The one open row of Schedule/DESIGN.md's §3 lemma table (everything else in it is proved).

STATEMENT: Sched K = colim C ≃ hocolim C ≃ |N(Ch K)|.
HYPOTHESIS: Ch K thin.
DESIGN.md calls it "near-definitional".

CRITICAL — use the RIGHT machinery. The Čech nerve lemma does NOT apply:
L7 (cones have meets) is FALSE. For the two-squares K, C[S] ∩ C[S'] is a union of TWO DISJOINT
half-planes ([S] and [S'] have two incomparable minimal common refinements and no meet). So the
cone family is not closed under intersection, intersections may be disconnected, and the Čech
nerve of that example acquires a chord and two filled triangles — giving a contractible complex
where the answer is S¹.

The correct machinery is the HOMOTOPY COLIMIT / projection lemma: every C a is contractible
(convex), and by L6 the fibre poset {a | x ∈ C a} is a principal up-set for every x, hence
contractible. L6 is the load-bearing lemma — the finest-chain map earns its keep as the
hypothesis of the hocolim theorem, not as IsLeast.

BLOCKER: mathlib has no nerve lemma, no homotopy colimit, and no geometric realization of a
category. That is why nothing here is stated as a theorem.

DONE WHEN: proved, or an explicit decision that it is out of reach until mathlib grows the machinery.
